# Pylon — Slack-First B2B Support

Pylon is a customer support platform purpose-built for B2B companies managing enterprise customer relationships through Slack Connect. Its primary value proposition: your enterprise customers already live in Slack; Pylon turns those shared Slack channels into a structured support inbox with SLA tracking, assignment, and (limited) AI assistance.

## Key findings

**Primary use case:** Pylon is optimal for B2B companies where every enterprise customer has a dedicated Slack Connect channel (the "CSM pod" model). It aggregates all those channels into a single support inbox, assigns conversations to agents, and tracks SLA across channels. This is distinct from Plain, which handles a mix of email + Slack + in-app messages.

**AI features (Pylon AI):** Pylon has a documented "Pylon AI" feature that surfaces suggested responses based on prior conversations and knowledge-base content. Benchmark resolution rates are not publicly available (as of May 2026). Practitioner reports on G2 suggest it reduces draft time rather than achieving autonomous resolution. Treat Pylon AI as a copilot, not an autonomous deflection agent.

**Linear integration:** Pylon has a Linear integration for escalating Slack-originated threads to Linear issues. The integration is documented but simpler than Plain's (no automatic thread resolution when Linear issue closes — this is a manual step).

**Email support:** Pylon's email inbox support is secondary to its Slack inbox. If a significant portion of your customer support comes via email (> 30% of volume), Pylon's UX degrades relative to Plain or Help Scout.

**Pricing:** Pylon's pricing is seat-based and not publicly listed; requires a demo. G2 reviewers report starting prices around $75-100/seat/month for the base plan. Comparable to Plain's Pro tier.

**Team size fit:** Pylon is best suited for companies with 5-50 enterprise customers in Slack Connect channels and a 2-10 person support/CS team. It becomes unwieldy at > 100 Slack channels without dedicated CS ops.

## Key takeaways

- Pylon is the right tool when your support model is "every customer has a Slack Connect channel" and you need structured inbox management for those channels.
- If you also have high email volume or in-app chat, Plain handles multi-surface better than Pylon.
- Pylon AI is a copilot feature, not an autonomous deflection agent — do not position it as an Intercom Fin equivalent.
- The Linear integration exists but is less automated than Plain's; factor in manual resolution steps.
- Pricing is opaque; recommend a demo call before committing; expect seat-based pricing comparable to Plain Pro.
